human_edited_text: Campaign-choice adoption averaged 61.9% during months 1–12, with a recurring substitution pattern: Midwest and Southwest suburban High-Value and Lifestyle Affinity customers recommended for Performance Collection frequently received New Season Edit instead. Modified executions converted worse than adopted recommendations. After the default-plan business action and v1.1 boundary tuning, campaign-choice adoption rose to 80.9% (+19.0% pts) and mean monthly net incremental revenue changed by $885. Marketing leadership agreed the default-plan control is the primary lever; model boundary work is secondary.
edit_reason: Clarified owner accountability and sequencing of business vs model actions.

human_edited_text: Southeast Performance Collection conversion fell outside the recent regional baseline in March 2025. A recorded business exception shows creative was delayed nine days, reducing valid send window coverage. This is labeled a business exception rather than a model defect.
edit_reason: No edit — confirmed as operational exception.

human_edited_text: Follow-up adoption improved in the post-intervention window, yet incremental revenue attribution for follow-up-only completions remains mixed versus campaign-choice gains. Current measurement classifies the dedicated follow-up coaching action as inconclusive.
edit_reason: Awaiting one more measurement month before classification.
